g.text: HMAC-SHA-256 = H(K⊕opad || H(K⊕ipad || msg)) — 2 complete SHA-256 passes
SHA-256 Pass 1: Inner Hash H(K⊕ipad || message)
SHA-256 Pass 2: Outer Hash H(K⊕opad || inner)
0x00 | uint32_t[8] | H // running hash state (32 bytes)
0x20 | uint8_t[64] | buf // partial block buffer (64 bytes)
0x60 | size_t | buf_len // valid bytes in buf [0..63]
0x68 | uint64_t | total_bits // input bits seen across all update() calls
Total: 104 bytes on stack — zero heap allocation
3 | sha256_update(ctx, msg, msg_len) | message: N blocks compressed
4 | sha256_finalize(ctx, inner) | pad + final compress + serialize
6c| sha256_finalize(ctx, mac) | pad(32+64 bits) + compress + done
= 2 complete SHA-256 computations | HMAC is a 10-line wrapper
to extend mac, need K⊕opad as prefix
K is secret → opad is secret
